title: LHC anomaly shakes the Standard Model as AI reshapes medicine - TCR 04/25/26
description:
A four-sigma anomaly at the LHC challenges the Standard Model, Google commits $40 billion to Anthropic, Isomorphic Labs moves AI-designed drugs to human trials, and the FDA approves a gene therapy that restores hearing in deaf children.
This episode covers the LHCb B meson decay deviation, Google's circular-economics investment in Anthropic, Isomorphic Labs clinical trials, Regeneron's free gene therapy for hearing loss, FDA psychedelic priority vouchers, Google's 75% AI-generated code disclosure, and the "too dangerous to release" pattern across frontier models.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-25-2026/

0:00 Google $40 billion Anthropic investment and circular economics
3:36 Gene therapy restores hearing and Regeneron offers it free
8:28 Anthropic compute constraints and IPO timeline
11:38 Google 75% AI-generated code and shifting developer roles
13:56 "Too dangerous to release" as industry norm
16:09 What shipped

The LHCb experiment reported both angular distributions and decay rates in an electroweak penguin process diverging from Standard Model predictions at 1-in-16,000 odds, with independent CMS results pointing in the same direction and three times more data already collected. Google announced up to $40 billion in cash and compute for Anthropic at a $350 billion valuation, creating an entanglement where its two largest investors are also its two sole infrastructure suppliers and two direct competitors. Isomorphic Labs confirmed AI-designed drug molecules are entering human clinical trials in oncology and immunology, with claims that computational protein understanding yields lower-dose, higher-precision medicines. The FDA approved Regeneron's Otarmeni gene therapy for genetic hearing loss - with free treatment for all eligible U.S. patients - and issued priority vouchers to three companies studying psilocybin and methylone for depression and PTSD. Google disclosed 75% of new company code is now AI-generated, up from 25% eighteen months ago. Time published an analysis framing restricted release as an emerging industry norm across Mythos, GPT-5.4-Cyber, and GPT-Rosalind.
